changeRequest.title: Add Real-Time Collaboration to Focus Mode
changeRequest.description: During sprint 3 development, user research revealed that 68% of target users work in team environments where shared focus sessions are critical. The current Focus Mode design (EPIC-3) only supports individual sessions. This change proposes adding real-time collaboration capabilities to Focus Mode, allowing teams to synchronize focus sessions, share ambient soundscapes, and see team members' focus status.
changeRequest.source: User research findings from beta testing cohort 2, validated by customer advisory board feedback

affectedEpics.impactDescription: Core Focus Mode stories need to account for multiplayer session state. S-3.1 (Focus Mode Engine) requires WebSocket support for real-time sync. S-3.2 (Ambient Soundscapes) needs shared playback state. A new story may be needed for the collaboration UI.

Integration Platform work will be pushed back by approximately 1 week as the team focuses on the collaboration features in EPIC-3. Calendar integration (S-4.1) timeline shifts from sprint 5 to sprint 6.

affectedStories.impactDescription: Must add WebSocket-based session synchronization for team focus sessions. Acceptance criteria need updating to include multi-user session state management, presence indicators, and graceful disconnection handling.
Shared soundscape playback requires a session leader model where one user controls the soundscape and others sync. Need to add acceptance criteria for synchronized audio state.
Delayed by 1 sprint to accommodate collaboration feature work. No functional changes, only timeline impact.

architectureImpact.description: Real-time collaboration requires WebSocket infrastructure that was not in the original architecture. Need to add a WebSocket gateway service, presence management, and session state synchronization protocol. The existing event-driven architecture can accommodate this with a new WebSocket adapter layer.
affectedComponents: Frontend State Management (new real-time sync layer)

timelineImpact.estimatedDelay: 1 week for EPIC-4 start; EPIC-3 extended by 3 days

resourceImpact.additionalEffort: Approximately 5 story points additional across EPIC-3
resourcesNeeded: Backend engineer with WebSocket experience (already on team)
Additional QA time for multi-user testing scenarios

riskAssessment.risk: WebSocket infrastructure adds latency to focus session state sync
riskAssessment.mitigation: Use optimistic UI updates with eventual consistency; implement local-first state with conflict resolution

riskAssessment.risk: Multi-user testing complexity delays EPIC-3 completion beyond estimate
riskAssessment.mitigation: Timebox collaboration features to 5 days; if not complete, ship individual focus mode first and add collaboration in a follow-up sprint

proposal.rationale: The user research data strongly supports this change — 68% of users want team focus sessions, and this aligns with our competitive differentiation strategy. However, we should limit the initial scope to basic session sync and presence, deferring shared soundscape control to a later sprint to minimize timeline impact.

options.description: Full collaboration suite: session sync, shared soundscapes, team presence, and collaborative task lists within focus sessions
Scoped collaboration: session sync and team presence only. Shared soundscape control deferred to sprint 6.
pros: Can iterate based on real usage data
cons: Doesn't fully address shared soundscape request
Users may expect more collaboration features

options.description: Defer entirely: ship individual Focus Mode as planned, add collaboration in sprint 7
cons: Misses market window — competitor launching team focus feature in March

storiesToAdd.title: Team Focus Session Sync
storiesToAdd.description: As a team member, I want to join a shared focus session so that my team and I can focus together and see each other's focus status in real time.

storiesToModify.modifications: Add acceptance criteria for WebSocket connection management, session join/leave events, and presence state broadcasting

documentsToUpdate.updates: Add WebSocket gateway service, session state sync protocol, and presence management component to architecture diagram
Add multi-user testing scenarios for concurrent focus sessions

proposal.rollbackPlan: If WebSocket infrastructure proves too complex within the 5-day timebox, revert to individual-only Focus Mode and ship collaboration as a separate epic in the next quarter. The WebSocket gateway can be feature-flagged for easy rollback.

approval.approvedBy: Product Manager — Sarah Chen, Tech Lead — Marcus Rivera
approval.approvalNotes: Approved Option B (scoped collaboration). Team agreed to the 5-day timebox constraint. Shared soundscape sync deferred to sprint 6 backlog.
conditions: Must stay within 5 story points additional effort
Architecture document updated before implementation begins
Multi-user test plan reviewed by QA lead before development starts

implementation.implementedBy: Backend: Marcus Rivera, Frontend: Aisha Patel
implementation.notes: WebSocket gateway scaffolding complete. Presence service unit tests passing. Frontend integration scheduled for Feb 25.
